response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software leadership across 20 years of experience and proven mentorship capabilities, there is a critical skills gap for this C++ and industrial-automation-focused role. The last substantial C++ work was 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015; the subsequent decade has focused on TypeScript/Node.js web technologies, D-lang, and full-stack development. The role requires deep C++ expertise combined with industrial automation, IoT, and IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle knowledge—all entirely absent from this resume. The trajectory shows progression away from systems programming, not toward it.
strengths: Extensive senior-level software engineering across 20 years with multiple staff roles, demonstrating architectural thinking, systems design, and leadership credibility
Strong mentorship and code-quality culture—founded testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), organized workshops on TDD and testing, and practiced software craftsmanship throughout career
Full-stack capability and proven ability to ship complex systems (GIS platforms, distributed data pipelines, real-time systems), showing technical breadth and execution
concerns: Critical C++ expertise gap: only 6 months of documented C++ work in 2014–2015; last 10+ years focused on TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript, and D-lang, with C++ absent from current skills list
No industrial automation, IoT, machinery, embedded systems, or manufacturing domain experience; background is web platforms, GIS, data systems, and fintech—misaligned with role requirements
Missing security and compliance expertise: no mention of SDL, IEC 62443, secure development lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards critical to this position

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and IoT domain absence
